translated: I will not be a fool, I will not be a slave.  Go, so that you may be.

translated: You are a divine being, oh, “I am the God of the Hebrews, I have heard your cry and I have seen your suffering. Now, go and tell Pharaoh to let my people go.”

ref: While Jacob and his family were traveling through Canaan, God terrified the people in the towns so much that no one dared bother them.

translated: When they arrived at the place, Korawizi said to them, "I will give you a gift, but you must first tell me what you want."

text: di ra bagli hɔpizaa ni bɔr biraa, di kaa bargi bɔchaaŋ ni bɔbiri la ge dɔŋa ni. Kɔraawiizi binnaa, di a wesso.

ref: They went back to the city of Enmishpat, better known as Kadesh. Then they captured all the land that belonged to the Amalekites, and they defeated the Amorites who were living in Hazazon-Tamar.

translated: These are the days that the Lord has made, let us rejoice and be glad in them. For the Lord has made them, let us rejoice and be glad in them, for the Lord has made them, let us rejoice and be glad in them.

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”

translated: The man said, “The Philistines came to Jacob. They fought with the Israelites, and they defeated them, and they took them captive.”

ref: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
text: Aizik jaa lau Ribɛka zuu u maa tanti dia, u jaa birgi u haaŋ. U bɔra sɔɔni, u bampirii jaa tig u maa siu la wia.
translated: Aizik went to Ribɛka's house and they talked together. He brought her a present and gave it to her. He said to her, "I love you, and I want to marry you."

ref: But he said, “Don't make me stay any longer. The Lord has already helped me find a wife for my master's son. Now let us return.”
text: Ka a balii bir ŋɔ, “Ií gilɛɛ laŋŋiŋ. Kɔraawiizi gillɔɔ wiimaa kuari. Awia, ií taŋ ŋmɛnɛɛ ń bir la n dɔɔri be.”
translated: He said to me, “I have seen the light. The one who is wise will understand.  Listen, I will tell you a secret.”

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Ŋɔ u be lugɔ u balii Jekɔb kunaa u kpa di ra ba ú wa tɛɛ u dɔɔri Ɛsau. U haa du harbɔɔ di ra ba.”

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Ɛsau haa vɛɛ suŋŋuu, di ŋɔ, “Amɔ, kissikpaŋ bɛnnɛ i dɔɔ di ra kissi nɛraa? N mɛ, kissi n ge gba!” Uŋ wa ŋɔ guŋ tuŋ, u ra chɛɛhgi.
translated: Esau said to Jacob, “Am I not more deserving of your father’s blessing than you? I am the older brother! Give me the blessing!” Jacob was angry with him, and he was afraid.

ref: After this, they took the coat to their father and said, “We found this! Look at it carefully and see if it belongs to your son.”

translated: When I went to the market, I saw a man and asked him, “Is this the place where they sell the best things? I want to buy Joseph’s goods, where are they?”

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: I ni a haaŋ daŋ dɔm, ií biizi ge dɔm. U bie daŋ chanzi i nyuu, i ge dum u naalume.”
translated: If you are going to speak, speak the truth. Do not speak lies. If you are going to tell a story, tell the truth.

ref: “How much do you want me to pay you?” Laban asked. Then Jacob told him: I don't want you to pay me anything. Just do one thing, and I'll take care of your sheep and goats.
text: Laban pɛɛzuu, “N turgii aŋmɛnɛi?” Ka Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Gilɛɛ tɛŋ kummaa. Ama, di i daŋ ɛɛ wikpaŋ naa tɛŋ, n daŋ haa ra binni i sela ni.
translated: Laban said, “Where are you going?” Jacob replied, “I am going to a distant place. I will not stay here any longer, for I must go to my own land.”

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Ŋɔ u be lugɔ u balii Jekɔb kunaa u kpa di ra ba ú wa tɛɛ u dɔɔri Ɛsau. U haa du harbɔɔ di ra ba.”
translated: And Jacob said, "I will surely give you the blessing, for you are my firstborn, and I will make you great and give you many descendants. You shall be ruler over your brothers, and all the people shall bow down to you. But Esau said, "I will kill you, Jacob."

ref: The Lord said to himself, “I should tell Abraham what I am going to do,

translated: Did Korah and his company say, "Is it not enough for you that we have brought you to this place, to this land that Abraham was promised?"

ref: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.

translated: The woman went to the market to buy things, and she met a man. She greeted him and he responded to her greeting.

ref: Then he said, “Please tell me who your father is. Does he have room in his house for me and my men to spend the night?”

translated: The little bird is singing a beautiful song in the morning. It sings so sweetly that it makes my heart happy.

text: U dɔɔ nauna, kauzi, peeza, buuna ani balaa kpila maanii filaŋkeŋ. Awiarɛ u tili ŋɔ ya wa kalii lugɔ u ra bau. Ka u ra suŋŋiibuu lugɔ í lɛɛu ani bɔsɔna.”
translated: When you are in trouble, seek help from your friends, your family, your neighbors and your relatives.  If you are in trouble, go to the elders and ask for their advice. Go to the elders and ask for their help.

ref: I created humans to be like me, and I will punish any animal or person that takes a human life. If an animal kills someone, that animal must die. And if a person takes the life of another, that person must be put to death.
